description: Most relevant base data around health: illness and disease, wellbeing, mortality and morbidity, life expectancy and the health-adjusted life expectancy (HALE), and the epidemiology measures prevalence, incidence and the health-adjusted life years (HALY) such as the QALY and DALY, drawn from published science. The formulas reproduce three relationships in the calc-validation block: the global health gap as life expectancy minus HALE (WHO 2021), the steady-state identity prevalence = incidence times duration (Rothman, Modern Epidemiology) for an illustrative chronic disease, and the QALY as life years times the health-utility weight (Weinstein and Stason 1977). Base words owned by other files (world, DALY, percent, year) are re-declared name-only so the import does not overwrite their canonical description; the topic-specific words carry a Wikipedia ref plus the cleaned first sentence of the lead as the description.

words.name: health
words.description: a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.

words.name: disease
words.description: a particular abnormal condition that negatively affects the structure or function of all or part of an organism.

words.name: illness
words.description: a state of poor health; the subjective experience of being unwell

words.name: wellbeing
words.description: the state of being comfortable, healthy, or happy.

words.name: mortality
words.description: the number of deaths in a population, scaled to the size of that population per unit of time.

words.name: morbidity
words.description: the rate or condition of being diseased in a population; the prevalence or incidence of ill health

words.name: life expectancy
words.description: a statistical measure of the average time an organism is expected to live, based on the year of birth and other demographic factors.

words.name: HALE
words.description: the average number of years a person can expect to live in full health, adjusting for time lived in less than full health.

words.name: prevalence
words.description: the proportion of a population found to be affected by a medical condition at a specific time.

words.name: incidence
words.description: the rate of new cases of a condition in a population over a specified period of time.

words.name: duration
words.description: the average length of time a health condition lasts

words.name: QALY
words.description: a measure that combines the quality and the quantity of life lived, used to assess the value of medical interventions.

words.name: HALY
words.description: health-adjusted life year, an umbrella measure of disease burden combining the length and the quality of life, such as the QALY and the DALY

words.name: utility
words.description: the health-related quality-of-life weight of a state, from 0 (death) to 1 (full health)

words.name: life
words.description: a quality that distinguishes living organisms from inorganic matter, marked by growth, reproduction and response to stimuli.

words.name: treatment
words.description: the attempted remediation of a health problem, usually following a medical diagnosis.

words.name: chronic
words.description: persisting for a long time or constantly recurring

words.name: gap
words.description: the difference between a quantity and a reference level

triples.name: chronic disease
triples.description: a disease that is long-lasting or constantly recurring

triples.name: health gap
triples.description: the years of life lived in less than full health, as life expectancy minus HALE

triples.name: life year
triples.description: a year of life lived, the quantity weighted by health utility to give a QALY

sources.name: WHO Global Health Observatory 2021
sources.description: World Health Organization, Global Health Observatory: global life expectancy at birth about 71.3 years and health-adjusted life expectancy (HALE) about 61.9 years in 2021; global crude death rate about 8.5 per 1000.

sources.name: Rothman Modern Epidemiology 2008
sources.description: Rothman KJ, Greenland S, Lash TL (2008). Modern Epidemiology, 3rd ed. In a steady state the prevalence of a condition equals its incidence rate times its average duration.

sources.name: Weinstein Stason 1977 NEJM
sources.description: Weinstein MC, Stason WB (1977). Foundations of cost-effectiveness analysis for health and medical practices. New England Journal of Medicine 296:716-721. A QALY is a life year weighted by a health-utility factor between 0 and 1.
